et psallem in glōriā meā. ^ My heart is: psaltērium et cittara~; exsurgem dīlūculō. ^ Arise
my heart is ready~; I will sing: psaltery and harp~; I will arise in the morning early.

O God: ^ I will praise thee among the people
and I will sing unto thee among the nations

parātum cor meum~; cantābō: et usque ad nūbēs vēritās tua. ^ for thy mercy is great above the heavens
et psallem in glōriā meā. ^ My heart is: and thy truth even unto the clouds.
et super omnem terram glōria tua
O God: ^ Be thou exalted above the heavens
and thy glory over all the earth

2 ^ Parātum cor meum Deus: 7 ^ ut līberentur dīlectī tuī~; salvum fac dexterā tuā
parātum cor meum~; cantābō: et exaudī mē. ^ that thy beloved may be delivered~; save with thy right hand and hear me.

2 ^ Parātum cor meum Deus: 8 ^ Deus locūtus est in sanctō suō
O God: et convallem tabernāculōrum dīmētier. ^ God hath spoken in his holiness
and I will divide Sichem and I will mete out the vale of tabernacles.

et psallem in glōriā meā. ^ My heart is: et Efraim susceptiō capitis meī~; Jūda rex meus. ^ Gilead is mine
my heart is ready~; I will sing: and Ephraim the protection of my head~; Judah is my king.

2 ^ Parātum cor meum Deus: 10 ^ Mōāb lebēs speī meae~; in Idūmaeam extendem calceamentum meum~; mihī aliēnigenae amīcī factī sunt.~»^ Moab is the pot of my hope~; over Idumaea I will stretch out my shoe~; the aliens are become my friends."

my heart is ready~; I will sing: in virtūtibus nostrīs~? ^ Wilt not thou

parātum cor meum~; cantābō: quia vāna salūs hominis. ^ O grant us help from trouble
et psallem in glōriā meā. ^ My heart is: for vain is the help of man.

parātum cor meum~; cantābō: et ipse ad nihilum dēdūcet inimīcōs nostrōs. ^ Through God we shall do mightily
